rationale: X caught the one substantive bug ground truth reviewers flagged and got fixed — the piecewise_attn/None-attn_func crash when only one of flash_attn_func/flash_attn_varlen_func is present (RuixiangMa's comment, matched almost verbatim) — while Y's own validation pass explicitly signed off on that exact guard as 'matching existing pattern' and missed the downstream crash entirely. Y is otherwise more precise (duplication, coverage gaps, doc mismatches, fallback-chain doc nit are all solid and well-evidenced), but X's headline finding about version=1/2 'silently no-opping' appears to contradict the author's later clarification that these are Hub git-branch tags, not PEP440 specifiers, hurting X's precision. Neither review touches the docs/test-marker discussion thread that dominates the ground truth.
